message:
fix(palette,swarm): eliminate stale state bugs in palette reload/search and false prompt detection in auto-respawner

Three bugs fixed, all discovered during a fresh-eyes audit:

1. Palette ReloadMsg silently ignored empty command lists (bd-c36if):
   The ReloadMsg handler had a guard `if len(msg.Commands) > 0` that
   prevented clearing the palette when a reload delivered zero commands.
   This meant removing all custom palette commands from config would
   leave stale commands visible in the live palette UI. Removed the
   guard so `m.commands` is always assigned from the reload payload,
   and `updateFiltered()` always recalculates derived state. Added
   TestReloadMsgClearsCommandsWhenReloadedEmpty to cover the edge case.

2. Palette XF search kept stale error after successful search (bd-cpl1s):
   When an XFSearchResultsMsg arrived with results (no error), the
   handler never cleared `m.xfErr`. If a previous search had timed out
   or failed, the error banner persisted even after the next search
   succeeded, confusing users into thinking the latest search had also
   failed. Now `m.xfErr = nil` is set on the success path before
   assigning results. Added TestXFSearchResultsMsgClearsPreviousErrorOnSuccess.

3. Auto-respawner false shell-prompt detection (bd-igs35):
   isShellPrompt had a two-pass check on each line: first HasSuffix
   (correct), then a second Contains pass that matched prompt characters
   like `$` and `>` appearing anywhere inside the line. Text such as
   "some output$ more" or "writing report > output.txt" would falsely
   signal an idle shell, causing premature respawn flow progression.
   Removed the Contains fallback entirely — only the suffix-based check
   remains, which correctly requires the prompt character at the end of
   the line. Updated the "prompt in middle" test expectation from true
   to false and added a new "redirect symbol in text" case plus an
   integration-style test (TestAutoRespawnerKillWithFallbackPromptCharacterInsideLineStillForceKills)
   that verifies force-kill is invoked when the output merely contains
   prompt-like characters mid-line.

Beads tracker updated with bd-c36if, bd-cpl1s, bd-igs35, and bd-exlab.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
